subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Transition metals have high melting point due to their high enthalpy of atomisation. They form strong metallic bonds with unpaired electrons.

Melting point of Ag=961.8°C

Melting point of Cs=28.5°C

Melting point of Ga=30°C

Melting point of Hg=-38.83°C

Cs,Ga and Hg are liquid at or near room temperature.

Among the given options Ag has the maximum Melting point, as it belongs to transition metal series.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Electron gain enthalpy is the amount of energy released when an element accepts an electron. The process is endothermic.

correct order of electron gain enthalpy is  O<S>Se>Te

⇒ S>Se>Te>O

(Oxygen shows least electron gain enthalpy due to small size of atom)

Electronegativity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>⇒</mo></math>Increases. Electron gainenthalpy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>⇒</mo></math> Increases. subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: Across a period metallic strength decreases $\&$ down the group it increases

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
solution:
Statement I: Metallic character decreases from left to right across a period and increases down a group. K (Group 1, Period 4) is the most metallic element among these. Mg (Group 2, Period 3) is less metallic than K. Al (Group 13, Period 3) is less metallic than Mg. B (Group 13, Period 2) is a metalloid with the least metallic character. The order K > Mg > Al > B correctly represents decreasing metallic character. TRUE.

Statement II: Atomic radius compared to ionic radius depends on whether the ion is a cation or anion. For metals forming cations: atomic radius > cationic radius (e.g., Na > Na⁺). However, for nonmetals forming anions: atomic radius < anionic radius (e.g., Cl < Cl⁻). The statement claims atomic radius is ALWAYS greater for ANY element, which is incorrect because anions have larger radii than their parent atoms. FALSE.

Correct answer: Statement I is true but Statement II is false.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: Ionisation energy decreases with increase in the size of atom. Na, K, Rb belongs to alkali metal and down the group ionization energy decreases as size of atom increases down the group.  Among the given elements, Sc being small has the highest ionization energy (Na= 496, K=419; Sc =631 kJ mol-1)

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: In period 4, atomic radii decrease from left to right as nuclear charge increases while electron shell remains constant. K (potassium) is at the extreme left of period 4 metals and has the highest atomic radius. Br (bromine) is near the right end as a nonmetal and has the lowest atomic radius in this period. Therefore, the elements with highest and lowest atomic radii in period 4 are K and Br respectively.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
A is incorrect. F and Cl belong to the same group of the periodic table. The electron gain enthalpy usually becomes less negative on moving down a group. However, in this case, the value of the electron gain enthalpy of Cl is more negative than that of F. This is because the atomic size of F is smaller than that of Cl. 

B is correct (ionisation energy decreases down the group).

C is incorrect (Electronegativity is a fixed property of an element and is typically measured on scales such as the Pauling scale or the Mulliken scale.). 

D is incorrect. The oxides which behave as both acidic and basic oxides are called amphoteric oxides. . Here Al2O3 is amphoteric oxide and NO is neutral oxide.

Hence, A, C and D are incorrect.

solution: It is due to increase in ionisation enthalpy and increases in electron gain enthalpy, when one moves from left to right in a period.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
In a period the value of ionisation potential increases from left to right with breaks where the atoms have some what stable configuration. After removing an $e^{-}$ oxygen will acquire stable configuration. Hence $\mathrm{O}^{+}$has highest second ionisation enthalpy than $\mathrm{F}^{+}$and $\mathrm{N}^{+}$.
$\therefore$ correct order is $\mathrm{O}>\mathrm{F}>\mathrm{N}>\mathrm{C}$

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Ionization Enthalpy values indicate that the metal belongs to Ist group, since the second ionization enthalpy is very high.
(Q - only one valence electron)
Metal hydroxide will be of the type, MOH.

MOH 1 mol + HCl 1 mol →MCl+ H 2 O

MOH+ 1 2 H 2 SO 4 → 1 2 M 2 SO 4 + H 2 O

(1mol) 1 2 mol So, one mole of HCl is required to react with one mole of MOH.

So, 1 2 mole of H 2 SO 4  is required to react with one mole of MOH.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Correct order of ionisation energy will be : Na < Al < Mg < Si.

On going from left to right in periodic table effective nuclear charge increases due to which ionisation energy increases. But there is exception in order of Al and Mg, in case of Mg electron is removed from fulfilled 's' subshell due to which it has higher I.E.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: Reactivity with water increases on moving down the Goup from $\mathrm{Li}$ to $\mathrm{Cs}$ as $\mathrm{Li} < \mathrm{Na}$ $ < \mathrm{K} < \mathrm{Rb} < \mathrm{Cs}$. This is due to increase in electropositive character. Hence Rb reacts most vigorously with water.

subject: Chemistry

solution:
Elements with highest electronegativity $\rightarrow \mathrm{F}, \mathrm{O}$
Elements with largest atomic size $\rightarrow \mathrm{Fr}, \mathrm{Ra}$
Elements which shows properties of both metal and non-metals i.e. metalloids $\rightarrow \mathrm{Ge}$, As
Elements with highest negative electron gain enthalpy $\rightarrow \mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{S}$

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Gallium (Ga) remains as liquid inside boiling water, and the other elements mentioned in options can react with water.

Bromine is a halogen that reacts readily with water to form hydrobromic acid and hypobromous acid, which can further react to form bromic acid. Lithium and Caesium are alkali metals that react violently with water, producing hydrogen gas and the corresponding metal hydroxide.
